title: Write whole numbers in different forms
description: Practice converting numbers between standard, expanded, and written form.
topic_path: Writing whole numbers in written form
Write whole numbers in different forms

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the chart so all three forms equal the same number.**

Standard Form | Word Form | Expanded Form
- | - | -
[[☃ dropdown 2]] | Six thousand, five hundred forty-seven | [[☃ dropdown 1]]
hints:
Let's use a place value chart to write $\tealD{\text{six thousand,}}$ $\blueD{\text{five hundred}} \purpleD{\text{ forty-}}\greenD{\text{seven}}$ in standard form.

Thousands | Hundreds | Tens |  Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\tealD6$ | $\blueD5$ | $\purpleC4$ | $\greenD7$

Now, let's use a place value chart to write $6{,}547$ in expanded form.

Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: |:-:
$\tealD6$ | $\blueD5$ | $\purpleD4$ | $\greenD7$

$6{,}547=\tealD{6{,}000}+\blueD{500}+\purpleD{40}+\greenD{7}$

This is the completed chart:

Standard Form | Word Form | Expanded Form
- | - | -
$6{,}547$ | Six thousand, five hundred forty-seven | ${6{,}000}+{500}+{40}+7$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the chart so all three forms equal the same number.**

Standard Form | Word Form | Expanded Form
- | - | -
[[☃ dropdown 2]] | Eighteen thousand, thirty-nine| [[☃ dropdown 1]]
hints:
Let's use a place value chart to write eighteen thousand, thirty-nine in standard form.

Ten Thousands | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens |  Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\goldD1$ | $\tealD8$ | $0$ | $\purpleD3$ | $\greenD9$

Now, let's use a place value chart to write $18{,}039$ in expanded form.

Ten Thousands | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens |  Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\goldD1$ | $\tealD8$ | $0$ | $\purpleD3$ | $\greenD9$

$18{,}039=\goldD{10{,}000}+\tealD{8{,}000}+\purpleD{30}+\greenD{9}$

This is the completed chart:

Standard Form | Word Form | Expanded Form
- | - | -
$18{,}039$ | Eighteen thousand, thirty-nine | ${10{,}000}+{8{,}000}+{30}+9$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the chart so all three forms equal the same number.**

Standard Form | Word Form | Expanded Form
- | - | -
 [[☃ dropdown 1]]| [[☃ dropdown 2]] | $20{,}000+4{,}000+700+60+5$
hints:
Let's use a place value chart to write $\goldD{20,000}+\tealD{4,000}+\maroonD{700}+\purpleD{60}+\greenD{5}$ in standard form.

Ten Thousands | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens |  Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\goldD2$ | $\tealD4$ | $\maroonD{7}$ | $\purpleD6$ | $\greenD5$

Now, let's break our number into parts to write it in word form:
$\redD{24}{,}\blueD{765}$

$\redD{\text{Twenty-four thousand}}$ is to the left of the comma. 

 $\blueD{\text{Seven hundred sixty-five}}$ is to the right of the comma.

If we combine the parts, we write $24{,}765$ as twenty-four thousand, seven hundred sixty-five.

This is the completed chart:

Standard Form | Word Form | Expanded Form
- | - | -
$24{,}765$ | Twenty-four thousand, seven hundred sixty-five | ${20{,}000}+{4{,}000}+{700}+{60}+5$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the chart so all three forms equal the same number.**

Standard Form | Word Form | Expanded Form
- | - | -
 [[☃ dropdown 1]]| [[☃ dropdown 2]] | $300{,}000+1{,}000+800+90+9$
hints:
Let's use a place value chart to write $\redD{300,000}+\tealD{1,000}+\blueD{800}+\purpleD{90}+\greenD{9}$ in standard form.

Hundred Thousands | Ten Thousands | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens |  Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:| :-:
$\redD3$ | $0$| $\tealD1$ | $\blueD8$ | $\purpleD9$ | $\greenD9$

Now, let's break our number into parts to write it in word form:
$\goldD{301}{,}\maroonD{899}$

$\goldD{\text{Three hundred one thousand}}$ is to the left of the comma. 

 $\maroonD{\text{Eight hundred ninety-nine}}$ is to the right of the comma.

If we combine the parts, we write $301{,}899$ as three hundred one thousand, eight hundred ninety-nine.

This is the completed chart:

Standard Form | Word Form | Expanded Form
- | - | -
$301{,}899$ | Three hundred one thousand, eight hundred ninety-nine | ${300{,}000}+{1{,}000}+{800}+{90}+9$

choices.content: Three hundred ten thousand, eight hundred ninety-nine
Three hundred one thousand, eight hundred ninety-nine

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the chart so all three forms equal the same number.**

Standard Form | Word Form | Expanded Form
- | - | -
$5{,}704$ | [[☃ dropdown 1]] | [[☃ dropdown 2]]
hints:
Now, let's use a place value chart to write $5{,}704$ in expanded form.

Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: |:-:
$\tealD5$ | $\maroonD7$ | $0$ | $\greenD4$

$5{,}704=\tealD{5{,}000}+\maroonD{700}+\greenD{4}$

Now, let's break our number into parts to write it in word form:
$\goldD{5}{,}\blueD{704}$

$\goldD{\text{Five thousand}}$ is to the left of the comma. 

 $\blueD{\text{Seven hundred four}}$ is to the right of the comma.

If we combine the parts, we write $5{,}704$ as five thousand, seven hundred four.

This is the completed chart:

Standard Form | Word Form | Expanded Form
- | - | -
$5{,}704$ | Five thousand, seven hundred four | ${5{,}000}+{700}+4$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the chart so all three forms equal the same number.**

Standard Form | Word Form | Expanded Form
- | - | -
$796{,}332$ | [[☃ dropdown 1]] | [[☃ dropdown 2]]
hints:
Now, let's use a place value chart to write $796{,}332$ in expanded form.

Hundred Thousands | Ten Thousands | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens |  Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:| :-:
$\redD7$ | $\goldD9$| $\tealD6$ | $\blueD3$ | $\purpleD3$ | $\greenD2$

$796{,}332=\redD{700{,}000}+\goldD{90{,}000}+\tealD{6{,}000}+\blueD{300}+\purpleD{30}+\greenD{2}$

Now, let's break our number into parts to write it in word form:
$\tealD{796}{,}\maroonD{332}$

$\tealD{\text{Seven hundred ninety-six thousand}}$ is to the left of the comma. 

 $\maroonD{\text{Three hundred thirty-two}}$ is to the right of the comma.

If we combine the parts, we write $796{,}332$ as seven hundred ninety-six thousand, three hundred thirty-two.

This is the completed chart:

Standard Form | Word Form | Expanded Form
- | - | -
$796{,}332$ | Seven hundred ninety-six thousand, three hundred thirty-two. | ${700{,}000}+{90{,}000}+{6{,}000}+{300}+{30}+2$

choices.content: Seven hundred ninety-six thousand, three hundred thirty-two
Seven hundred sixty-nine thousand, three hundred thirty-two
Seven hundred ninety-three thousand, three hundred thirty-two

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which $2$ of the following are equivalent to "seven thousand, eight hundred twenty-one"?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's use a place value chart to write $\tealD{\text{seven thousand,}} \blueD{\text{ eight hundred}} \purpleD{\text{ twenty-}}\greenD{\text{one}}$ in standard form.

Thousands | Hundreds | Tens |  Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\tealD7$ | $\blueD8$ | $\purpleD2$ | $\greenD1$

In standard form, we write $\tealD{\text{seven thousand,}}$$\blueD{\text{ eight hundred}} \purpleD{\text{ twenty-}}\greenD{\text{one}}$ as $\tealD7{,}\blueD8\purpleD2\greenD1$.

Now, let's use a place value chart to write $7{,}821$ in expanded form.

Thousands | Hundreds | Tens | Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: |:-:
$\tealD7$ | $\blueD8$ | $\purpleD2$ | $\greenD1$

$7{,}821=\tealD{7{,}000}+\blueD{800}+\purpleD{20}+\greenD{1}$

The following are equivalent to seven thousand, eight hundred twenty-one:  

- $7{,}000+800+20 +1$
- $7{,}821$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which $2$ of the following are equivalent to "four hundred thirty thousand, nine hundred thirty-six"?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's use a place value chart to write four hundred thirty thousand, nine hundred thirty-six in standard form.

Hundred Thousands | Ten Thousands | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens |  Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:| :-:
$\redD4$ | $\goldD3$| $0$ | $\blueD9$ | $\purpleD3$ | $\greenD6$

In standard form, we write four hundred thirty thousand, nine hundred thirty-six as $\redD4\goldD30{,}\blueD9\purpleD3\greenD6$.

Now, let's use a place value chart to write $430{,}936$ in expanded form.

Hundred Thousands | Ten Thousands | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens |  Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:| :-:
$\redD4$ | $\goldD3$| $0$ | $\blueD9$ | $\purpleD3$ | $\greenD6$

$430{,}936=\redD{400{,}000}+\goldD{30{,}000}+\blueD{900}+\purpleD{30}+\greenD{6}$

The following are equivalent to four hundred thirty thousand, nine hundred thirty-six:  

- $400{,}000+30{,}000+900+30+6$
- $430{,}936$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which $2$ of the following are equivalent to $500{,}000+20{,}000+8{,}000+500+90+3$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's use a place value chart to write $\redD{500,000}+\goldD{20{,}000}+\tealD{8,000}+\blueD{500}+\purpleD{90}+\greenD{3}$ in standard form.

Hundred Thousands | Ten Thousands | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens |  Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:| :-:
$\redD5$ | $\goldD2$| $\tealD8$ | $\blueD5$ | $\purpleD9$ | $\greenD3$

Now, let's break our number into parts to write it in word form:
$\tealD{528}{,}\maroonD{593}$

$\tealD{\text{Five hundred twenty-eight thousand}}$ is to the left of the comma. 

 $\maroonD{\text{Five hundred ninety-three}}$ is to the right of the comma.

If we combine the parts, we write $528{,}593$ as five hundred twenty-eight thousand, five hundred ninety-three.

The following are equivalent to $500{,}000+20{,}000+8{,}000+500+90+3$:  

- Five hundred twenty-eight thousand, five hundred ninety-three
- $528{,}593$

choices.content: Five hundred twenty-three thousand, five hundred ninety-eight
Five hundred twenty-eight thousand, five hundred ninety-three

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which $2$ of the following are equivalent to $70{,}000+1{,}000+600+4$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's use a place value chart to write $\goldD{70{,}000}+\tealD{1,000}+\blueD{600}+\greenD{4}$ in standard form.

Ten Thousands | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens |  Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\goldD7$| $\tealD1$ | $\blueD6$ | $0$ | $\greenD4$

Now, let's break our number into parts to write it in word form:
$\redD{71}{,}\purpleC{604}$

$\redD{\text{Seventy-one thousand}}$ is to the left of the comma. 

 $\purpleC{\text{Six hundred four}}$ is to the right of the comma.

If we combine the parts, we write $71{,}604$ as seventy-one thousand, six hundred four.

The following are equivalent to $70{,}000+1{,}000+600+4$:  

- Seventy-one thousand, six hundred four
- $71{,}604$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which $2$ of the following are equivalent to $85{,}121$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's use a place value chart to write $85{,}121$ in expanded form.

Ten Thousands | Thousands | Hundreds | Tens |  Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-
$\goldD8$| $\tealD5$ | $\redD{1}$ | $\purpleD2$ | $\greenD1$

$85{,}121=\goldD{80{,}000}+\tealD{5{,}000}+\redD{100}+\purpleD{20}+\greenD{1}$

Now, let's break our number into parts to write it in word form:
$\maroonD{85}{,}\blueD{121}$

$\maroonD{\text{Eighty-five thousand}}$ is to the left of the comma. 

 $\blueD{\text{One hundred twenty-one}}$ is to the right of the comma.

If we combine the parts, we write $85{,}121$ as eighty five thousand, one hundred twenty-one.

The following are equivalent to $85{,}121$:  

- Eighty five thousand, one hundred twenty-one
- $80{,}000+5{,}000+100+20+1$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which $2$ of the following are equivalent to $2{,}404$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's use a place value chart to write $2{,}404$ in expanded form.

Thousands | Hundreds | Tens |  Ones
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
$\tealD2$ | $\purpleC{4}$ | $0$ | $\greenD4$

$2{,}404=\tealD{2{,}000}+\purpleC{400}+\greenD{4}$

Now, let's break our number into parts to write it in word form:
$\goldD{2}{,}\blueD{404}$

$\goldD{\text{Two thousand}}$ is to the left of the comma. 

 $\blueD{\text{Four hundred four}}$ is to the right of the comma.

If we combine the parts, we write $2{,}404$ as two thousand, four hundred four.

The following are equivalent to $2{,}404$:  

- Two thousand, four hundred four
- $2{,}000+400+4$
